speaker: narrator
text: Otto and the Sheriff have retired for the evening.

speaker: sheriff
text: The Sheriff calls out behind you:  "Just a friendly suggestion, but whatever you do, don't drink the Dragon's Breath.  Not even Otto can drink Dragon's Breath!"
say: Just a friendly suggestion, but whatever you do, don't drink the Dragon's Breath.  Not even Otto can drink Dragon's Breath!

speaker: narrator
text: There's no hint of the Ogre.

speaker: narrator
text: You see no sign of the Sheriff.

speaker: narrator
text: A typical barber's pole.

speaker: narrator
text: It is closed, locked, and barred.

speaker: narrator
text: There's a sign on the door of the barber shop, informing whoever reads it that the barber is out to lunch.  The door is locked.

speaker: narrator
text: The barber must keep odd lunch hours.

speaker: narrator
text: There appears to be a note on the door of the barber shop.

speaker: narrator
text: You'd better not. The Goon would probably try to strangle you with his yo-yo if he thought you were going into the Sheriff's office without permission.

speaker: narrator
text: There's a sign on the door of the barber's shop, informing whoever reads it that the barber is out to lunch. The door is locked.

speaker: narrator
text: The gate is already closed.

speaker: narrator
text: The sturdy town gate is completely locked and barred, as it is every evening.

speaker: narrator
text: Only the Sheriff can unlock the gate.

speaker: narrator
text: The Barber Shop appears to be closed. There's a note hanging from the doorknob.

speaker: narrator
text: The man with the pipe greets you.

speaker: sheriff
text: "Welcome to our town. You are lucky to have made it down from the mountains before the snow blocked the pass again. It's gotten pretty dangerous outside of town, I understand."

speaker: sheriff
text: "Many monsters have been trapped around here with the late snow. Between them and the brigands, we certainly could use a Hero around here."

speaker: sheriff
text: "I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson. This is Otto Von Goon, my assistant. What do you call yourself? "

speaker: narrator
text: There's more than a hint of Ogre about this strange and bulky character. He seems cheerful, though.

speaker: narrator
text: Schultz appears to be an affable old coot, content to smoke his pipe. There's a hardness behind his eyes, however, that gives you reason to believe that he's no pushover.

speaker: narrator
text: It goes up and down...up and down...up and down...up and down.....

speaker: narrator
text: The Sheriff's pipe is a truly impressive hand-carved ivory pipe.

speaker: narrator
text: The Sheriff informs you that he's much too busy for idle chit-chat.

speaker: narrator
text: Otto only expresses himself physically.

speaker: sheriff
text: "Just walk around and find out for yourself."

speaker: sheriff
text: "You'll need to talk to the Baron about that. Or go ask Wolfgang down at the Guild Hall, which is just down the street."

speaker: sheriff
text: "So maybe you're going to be a hero? Well, we could surely use one around here, what with all the brigands."

speaker: sheriff
text: "There's a whole band of brigands hiding out somewhere in the mountains around us. They robbed a merchant just last week. Got a fair amount of treasure, I hear."

speaker: sheriff
text: "I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson, and this is Otto Von Goon, my assistant."

speaker: sheriff
text: The Sheriff replies, "Why, that's me!"

speaker: sheriff
text: "The one who was robbed? He's staying at the Inn, I believe."

speaker: sheriff
text: "Go talk to the Innkeeper. He will help you there."

speaker: sheriff
text: "You should know. You came down from there."

speaker: sheriff
text: "My friend," the Sheriff says, "this world's filled with dangers. Sometimes I think it might be better to choose your danger, rather than sitting around, waiting for it, as I seem to do."
say: My friend, this world's filled with dangers. Sometimes I think it might be better to choose your danger, rather than sitting around, waiting for it, as I seem to do.

speaker: sheriff
text: "But enough of this philosophical rambling. I am content enough to be Sheriff of Spielburg."

speaker: sheriff
text: "It's simple. When somone breaks the law, and we catch them, we hold them prisoner."

speaker: sheriff
text: "Obtaining treasure involves putting yourself in danger. If you're brave, foolish, or lucky, you might get some treasure."

speaker: sheriff
text: "The monsters come down from the mountains every winter, but they're usually gone by now. The Baron just doesn't have enough men to handle them."

speaker: sheriff
text: "Wolfgang is the one to talk to about monsters. He's had a lot of experience with them."

speaker: sheriff
text: "Baron Stefan keeps mostly to himself at his castle north of here. We haven't seen him here in town for years."

speaker: sheriff
text: "Wolfgang, down at the Guild hall, is the one to talk to about monsters. He's had a lot of experience with them."

speaker: sheriff
text: "Otto is a big help to me," the Sheriff tells you. "He's very smart for a Goon. His prisoners only suffer a few broken bones when he grabs them."
say: Otto is a big help to me. He's very smart for a Goon. His prisoners only suffer a few broken bones when he grabs them.

speaker: sheriff
text: "It took a bit of doing to teach him to bring prisoners back alive, but he hardly ever forgets, now."

speaker: sheriff
text: "The Guild Hall is at the Southwest corner of town. If you're looking for work, that's a good place to go."

speaker: sheriff
text: "Just next to the Inn is a small Magic Shop. The owner, Zara, will let you in if you have some abilities in that area. She's a strange one, all right!"

speaker: sheriff
text: The Sheriff says, "You think I'm going to tell you everything I know about everything? Go look around and find things out for yourself."
say: You think I'm going to tell you everything I know about everything? Go look around and find things out for yourself.

speaker: narrator
text: However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over and your wallet feels very light.

speaker: sheriff
text: "As safe as our streets are, you still should not sleep on them.  It is a good way to get robbed."
